speaker: interviewer
text: Eight interview with Mr. Zoltan Wieder. Could you tell us about this picture please?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my grandfather's picture. His name is Eliezer Vider. And Yiddish is Eliezer or the Hebrew name. His Hungarian or Romanian name is Lazar, first name. His last name is Vider. And that picture was taken, I think it was-- I don't know what exact year it was taken. You But probably it was around-- because he died in 1936. He was 76 years old. So it was taken, I'm sure, a few years before. And we got this picture-- this picture, they found my cousin, who lives in Venezuela. His name is Alex [NON-ENGLISH]. He found it in London. In London, my grandfather had a relative. And they had that picture there. So we got this picture.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: She is my grandmother. Her name is Blima Vider. I don't know her maiden name. And that picture was taken-- because I didn't know her before. She died before I was born. And that picture probably was taken, it looks like before 1925. So and we found that picture also in England through my cousin, Alex [NON-ENGLISH]. And they had a relative there.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is my father and my mother's picture. That was taken probably-- they were very young and maybe before I was born. I think after the wedding, maybe a few years after the wedding. And my father's name is Yehoshua Vider, or Samuel Vider. And my mother's name is Regina Vider or [YIDDISH] Vider.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where did you get it?

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. We got this picture-- we got the small picture, we found-- I found in Romania small pitcher after I went back to-- after I went back from the concentration camp. And I bought it out here, and we made it big-- we enlarged it here.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know where it was taken, in what city?

speaker: interviewee
text: The picture was taken in Baia-Sprie in Romania.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my sister's picture, my older sister's picture, which actually she is two years-- she was two years younger than me. That was taken when she was 15 years, 15 and a half, something like this, about a year before the concentration-- before they took her to concentration camp. It was taken in Baia-Sprie, Romania. I found this picture when I went back to Romania. And we made copies here. We enlarged this here in America.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a certificate, which I got in Dachau from the American commander, military commander in Dachau. You can see that his name is Sutton. And I got it in September 29, 1945.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was it--

speaker: interviewee
text: July. Sorry. July 29, 1945, in Dachau. They gave me that certificate, which says I came from Warsaw to the-- from Warsaw concentration camp to Dachau. And I was liberated in [NON-ENGLISH]. This is my brother and my sister-in-law, Adolph Wieder and [YIDDISH] Wieder. It was taken in Baia-Sprie in Romania around 1950, after the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my wedding picture. It was taken in Romania on August 13, 1961. My fiance, my, my wife now, her name is-- her maiden name is Eva Klein. This is a copy of my birth certificate. It was made, actually, the copy in 1966, in Romania. We chose the registration when I was born in Baia-Sprie, Romania. It also shows that my father and my mother's name. This is my wedding registration certificate that shows I was married in 1961 in Baia-Mare. Actually, the official marriage certificate is August 10th. And the religious marriage was August 13th. This is a picture. It was taken in Baia-Sprie in front of my parents' house. You can see it's very bad condition and not maintained. And we were there visiting. And I think this was taken in 1989 probably.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who's there with you?

speaker: interviewee
text: My wife is with me there. So we visited Baia-Sprie. We went to the cemetery, which I have my grandparents and my parents buried there.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Naturally, we visited our parent's house too. This is a picture that was taken in 1993, in Baia-Mare, where we used to live after the war. And I am with my wife there in front of our house. We built this house, except the top floor, which was added after that, after we left the country.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. So this is a new house?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a new house. I built it actually before I got married. And when we got married, we moved in there to this house.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in 1993. I am there with my wife in front of the city hall building. This building is very old. It could be about 200 years old. And right near to the building, which we can't see here, it was the school where I-- the primary school.

speaker: interviewer
text: What is the name of the building?

speaker: interviewee
text: The building is [HUNGARIAN]. In Hungarian, [HUNGARIAN]. And in Romanian, they say-- I don't know. I forgot. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: But-- Where is it?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's city hall actually.

speaker: interviewer
text: City hall where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Baia-Sprie.

speaker: interviewer
text: In Baia-Sprie. And who's-- that's your wife?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's my wife and myself. This is a picture which was taken in 1996, in New York. My brother is there.

speaker: interviewer
text: His name please.

speaker: interviewee
text: His name is Adolph Wieder. My sister-in-law, [YIDDISH] Wieder. And my niece, [YIDDISH] Wieder.

speaker: interviewer
text: Your niece is the one in the--

speaker: interviewee
text: In the black--

speaker: interviewer
text: --black dress.

speaker: interviewee
text: --dress.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was the occasion?

speaker: interviewee
text: The occasion was his 75th birthday.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was taken in their home. This is my daughter's wedding picture. There she is with her husband.

speaker: interviewer
text: And your daughter's name please.

speaker: interviewee
text: My daughter's name is Judith or Blima. In Yiddish, her name is Blima. And her husband there is Herschel. He is also called [NON-ENGLISH] Lampel. And that was taken-- that took place in 1981.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In New York, in Long Island.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: There are the three grandchildren. The girl is Chana [NON-ENGLISH]. She is now six and a half years old. The little boy-- the middle boy is Yehoshua. Now, he's four years old. And the little one is Eliezer. And now he is 20 months old.

speaker: interviewer
text: The little one is the one in the middle?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the middle, right. That's the little one.

speaker: interviewer
text: And their last name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Their last name in Lampel.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was taken in Passaic. They live in Passaic, New Jersey. It was taken in 1996.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. I want to thank you, again, Mr. Wieder for sharing your testimony. And we wish you well.

speaker: interviewee
text: You're welcome. And thank you very much.
